diagrams.title: Scanline Polygon Filling Algorithm
diagrams.description: A visual demonstration of the scanline fill algorithm showing horizontal scanlines crossing a polygon, with intersection points highlighted and the filled regions between paired intersections. This helps students understand how the algorithm processes the polygon row by row.
elements: Irregular polygon (pentagon or hexagon) with distinct colored edges
Multiple horizontal scanlines at regular y-intervals passing through the polygon
Red intersection points where scanlines cross polygon edges
Filled horizontal line segments between paired intersection points (highlighted in blue)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental scanline algorithm concept where pixels are filled by finding edge intersections along horizontal lines and filling between pairs of intersections from left to right

diagrams.title: Active Edge Table (AET) Structure
diagrams.description: A diagram showing a polygon divided into edges with an accompanying table structure that organizes edge information. The visualization demonstrates how the Active Edge Table is built and updated during scanline processing.
elements: Triangle or quadrilateral with numbered edges (E1, E2, E3, E4)
Table structure showing columns: Edge ID, y-min, y-max, x-intersection, inverse-slope (1/m)
Sorted edge entries at different y-positions
Curved arrows or flow indicators showing edge transition between scanlines
diagrams.purpose: Explains the Active Edge Table data structure used in efficient scanline rendering, showing how edge information (y-min, y-max, x-intersection, slope) is organized and updated at each scanline

diagrams.title: Boundary Fill Algorithm
diagrams.description: A step-by-step visualization of the boundary fill algorithm showing a polygon with a boundary color and a seed point inside. Shows the fill process expanding from the seed to fill the interior while being constrained by the boundary.
elements: Polygon outline with distinct boundary color (e.g., green border)
Seed point marked inside the polygon (red dot)
Progressively filled regions showing the expansion pattern
Legend explaining boundary color vs fill color
Arrows or numbering showing fill sequence/order
diagrams.purpose: Explains the seed-based boundary fill algorithm where starting from an interior point, adjacent pixels are recursively filled until reaching the boundary color, demonstrating the flood-fill concept used in paint applications
